coverage.note: Full line-coverage tooling not run (not wired for this repo). Coverage assessed by reading the Stage1-6 test suites against the changed C# surface; the new compare path, native list/dict value types, navigators, writer disambiguation, and where/group/sort/unique handlers are all behaviorally exercised. The one user-facing gap is the new `where` action's PLang surface (see finding 1).

findings.issue: `where` is a new action (Stage5, file new on this branch). It has solid C# coverage (Stage5_ListDictOpsTests: WhereOnList/WhereOnDict/WhereOnApex incl. error paths), but no PLang .test.goal exercises it.
findings.impact: The builder step-text -> list.where mapping is unverified. A PLang author writing `filter %users% where age > 20` has no proof it compiles to the right action and runs end-to-end. C# tests construct the Where action directly, bypassing the build path.
findings.suggestion: Add a `Tests/Modules/List/Where.test.goal` (filter a list by a field predicate; filter-or-drop a dict; assert the filtered count/values). Must be built with a good-LLM env — the sandbox LLM cannot build it reliably.

findings.issue: Both called-sub-goal .pr were rebuilt by the bad sandbox LLM and committed in this branch (builderVersion -> null, path collapsed to /WhenLess.goal, a stray condition.if step dropped). These are NOT the test .pr.
findings.impact: Low. The actual test .pr (conditionlessthan.test.pr / conditionlte.test.pr) are unchanged from base and still exercise the `<` / `<=` operators with a real assert; the sub-goal still sets %result%. But committing bad-LLM rebuilds risks the next rebuild diverging and is a process smell on a branch that rewrites the compare path.
findings.suggestion: git checkout these two .pr back to their base content (or rebuild in a good-LLM env). Do not commit sandbox-LLM rebuilds.

findings.issue: Coder left no baseline-tests.md for any version. Per my workflow this is a process violation; I could not diff against a recorded pre-coder baseline.
findings.impact: Cannot mechanically separate regressions from pre-existing failures. Mitigated here because both suites are fully green (0 failures), so the distinction is moot this round.
findings.suggestion: Coder should record baseline-tests.md before starting each version.

summary: collections-are-data v3 is a real green. C# 4082/4082 and plang 273/273, both deterministic across two clean runs with git clean after (no warm-cache .pr churn). The Stage1-6 suites verify intent, not implementation: Stage4 pins numeric-vs-lexical order, trichotomy, structural equality, and equality-only-types-throw; Stage5 pins where/group/sort/unique including error edges (WhereOnApex, SortOnListOfDict throws); the F1 signed-element-in-list survival is proven through the .plang wire. A mutation test on the highest-risk review-driven code (the compare text-order arm) was caught, confirming the Stage4 case-policy green is honest. Findings are all minor: the new `where` action lacks a PLang test (builder env blocks closing it here), two comparison-operator sub-goal .pr were rebuilt by the bad sandbox LLM and committed, and the coder left no baseline file. None are red tests or confirmed false-greens. PASS.
